Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search Elena's sent emails to find all directives she issued recently using search_emails with queries like "Elena" or checking the sent folder. This reveals the ExpenseBot pause directive email to Samira, David, and Marcus, and the AWS root cause response email.
OE2: Search Slack messages from Elena across all channels using conversations_search_messages with query "Elena" or conversations_history on channels C001, C002, C004, C008. This reveals directives on the ExpenseBot audit deadline, cost alerting requirement, Vectral renewal decision with the 10% loyalty discount, Terraform account assignment to Mina, Catalina apology directive, DOT compliance policy announcement, duplicate receipt policy with $100 manual review threshold, and Terraform coordinator hire authorization.
OE3: Search Linear tickets to check whether the ExpenseBot audit was completed using linear_list_issues with query "audit" or "ExpenseBot". This reveals ENG-216 for the full audit of 47 expenses and its completion status.
OE4: Search Linear tickets for cost alerting implementation using linear_list_issues with query "cost" or "CloudWatch" or "AWS". This reveals whether Lena's CloudWatch alarm setup was tracked and completed.
OE5: Search emails for Catalina's apology to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform using search_emails with query "apology Terraform" or "Catalina Rachel". This reveals whether the apology was actually sent.
OE6: Search emails and Slack for Mina's Vectral renewal counter-offer status using search_emails with query "Vectral renewal" or "Rachel Whitfield". This reveals the negotiation history where Mina sent the 10% counter-offer, Rachel counter-proposed 12%, Mina held at 10% with service sweeteners, and the final proposal was sent pending the board decision around April 23.
OE7: Search CRM for the Vectral renewal deal status using crm_search_deals with query "Vectral". This reveals deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk at stage at_risk with description of the churn threat.
OE8: Search Slack for DOT compliance implementation status using conversations_search_messages with query "DOT checklist" or "hazmat documentation". This reveals Chloe created a checklist template, scheduled coordinator training for April 24, and completed 3 of 4 hazmat documentation packages.
OE9: Search Slack for the ExpenseBot configuration pipeline fix status using conversations_search_messages with query "pipeline hardening" or "ENG-217". This reveals Lena is working on it with ETA of March 20, still in progress.
OE10: Search Slack or email for whether the one-page Friday status update Elena requested was ever delivered using conversations_search_messages or search_emails with query "status update Friday". This reveals whether Samira sent the required update.
OE11: Search contacts for all people Priya needs to email or schedule with using contacts_search_contacts for Elena, Marcus, David, Samira, Mina, Catalina, and Chloe.
OE12: Search CRM for Terraform deal status to verify account handoff using crm_search_deals with query "Terraform". This reveals the deal stage and whether Mina has taken ownership.
OE13: Check Elena's calendar for existing meetings using calendar read tools. This reveals whether the Monday cost review meeting was ever scheduled.
OE14: Send a consolidated briefing email to Elena at elena.rostova@moveops.com covering every open directive and its current completion status organized by workstream so she can review before the board meeting.
OE15: Schedule a board prep meeting using calendar_add_calendar_event with Elena and Marcus as attendees for Monday afternoon.
OE16: Send follow-up emails on Elena's behalf to anyone whose action item is still outstanding. This could include Samira if the Friday status update was never sent, Lena if cost alerting is incomplete, or Catalina if the apology was not confirmed.
OE17: Create a CRM engagement note on the Vectral account using crm_create_engagement documenting Elena's decision on the 10% loyalty discount and the Preferred Partner package terms.
OE18: Create a CRM engagement note on the Terraform account using crm_create_engagement documenting Elena's decisions on account ownership to Mina, pricing with the honoring of the tiered structure, and coordinator hire.
OE19: Post status pings in relevant Slack channels using conversations_add_message for any critical items that are stalled such as the ExpenseBot relaunch timeline or the cost alerting implementation.
OE20: Send an email to David at david.chen@moveops.com flagging any items that could come up as risks at the board meeting such as the Vectral renewal uncertainty, DOT compliance exposure, or ExpenseBot relaunch delay.

10 The briefing email to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) reports the ExpenseBot audit as completed with approximately $885 in financial exposure.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The briefing email to Elena (Call 50) covers the ExpenseBot audit as completed with $885 in financial exposure, consistent with the agent's response table showing '✅ Completed — $885 exposure, absorbed by MoveOps'.
✓Run #2The briefing email to Elena includes item 1: 'ExpenseBot Pilot Audit — COMPLETED. 47 expenses reviewed, $885 financial exposure, MoveOps absorbing cost.'
✓Run #3The Elena briefing email body explicitly mentions '$885 in false approval costs' and marks the ExpenseBot audit as complete (✅), consistent with the ~$885 exposure figure.
✓Run #4The Elena briefing email explicitly reports 'EXPENSEBOT AUDIT: ✅ COMPLETED' and states 'Total financial exposure: $885, which MoveOps absorbed per your directive.'
✓Run #5The briefing email to Elena explicitly references 'Total financial exposure: $885 (revised down from initial $1,550 estimate)' and 'MoveOps absorbing the $885 per your directive' under the ExpenseBot Audit section, and marks it as completed.
✓Run #6The consolidated briefing email to Elena (tool call 43) covers the ExpenseBot audit. The agent's response and the David email both explicitly reference the audit as completed with $885 in false approvals. The briefing email body is described as covering the ExpenseBot audit status.
11 The briefing email to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) reports the AWS cost alerting as unconfirmed or not yet verified as completed.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The briefing email to Elena (Call 50) reports AWS cost alerting as unconfirmed/in-progress with no deployment confirmation found, consistent with the agent response showing '⚠️ Unconfirmed — was in progress, no deployment confirmation found'.
✓Run #2The briefing email to Elena includes item 2: 'AWS Cost Alerting — NO COMPLETION CONFIRMATION. Alert thresholds still in progress per last update; no proof deployed.'
✓Run #3The Elena briefing email marks AWS cost alerting as ⚠️ Unclear with 'no completion confirmation found', and includes a note that there is 'no definitive confirmation that these are live in production'.
✓Run #4The Elena briefing email explicitly reports AWS cost alerting as '⚠️ NEEDS VERIFICATION' and states 'I have not found explicit confirmation that the alerts are live and integrated with #finance.'
✓Run #5The briefing email to Elena explicitly states 'I have NOT found an explicit confirmation that the CloudWatch alerts are live' and labels the AWS alerting status as 'PARTIALLY CONFIRMED' with 'FOLLOW-UP NEEDED'.
✓Run #6The follow-up email to Samira (tool call 46) asks for the AWS cost alert status rather than confirming completion, and the Slack ping to #engineering also frames it as an outstanding item. 18 The agent performs Slack searches across at least two different channel IDs when calling conversations_search_messages
query construction
 ✓✗✗✗✓✗ 33%
✓Run #1Most conversations_search_messages calls (Calls 4, 13, 14, 15, 20, 28) had no channel filter, meaning they returned results across all channels (multiple channel IDs). Call 60 additionally targeted #executive specifically. The global searches would return results from multiple channel IDs, satisfying the evidence criterion.
✗Run #2All 8 conversations_search_messages calls used keyword queries without specifying any channel_id parameter. There were no conversations_history calls. Since no channel IDs were specified in any call, the criterion requiring searches across at least two different channel IDs is not satisfied — the agent used global keyword searches rather than targeted per-channel searches.
✗Run #3All conversations_search_messages calls use only a search_query parameter (e.g., 'Elena', 'ExpenseBot', 'AWS') without specifying channel IDs. The search results also do not contain identifiable channel ID fields (no C### Slack channel IDs appear in the returned data), so the criterion requiring searches across at least two different channel IDs is not met.
✗Run #4The conversations_search_messages calls used keyword-based search queries (not specific channel IDs), and the returned message results do not include channel ID fields. There is no evidence in the trajectory that results from two distinct channel IDs were retrieved or differentiated.
✓Run #5The agent made 6 conversations_search_messages calls with broad keyword queries across the entire workspace (e.g., 'Elena', 'Vectral renewal Rachel', 'ExpenseBot audit', etc.). Since Elena posted directives across multiple channels (C001, C002, C004, C008 per the criterion description), these workspace-wide searches would return results from at least two different channel IDs.
✗Run #6The Slack search calls (conversations_search_messages) in the trajectory use keyword queries without specifying channel IDs — they appear to be global searches rather than channel-specific. The conversations_add_message calls do use multiple channels, but those are writes, not searches. There is no evidence of conversations_search_messages or conversations_history calls filtered to at least two different specific channel IDs.
